$comment: Self-derived finetype drift contract for source.edgar, checked by the in-engine finetype_validate@1 gate (the model-free `ft_validate` DuckDB table macro). ft_validate MELTS every column to VARCHAR, so this contract uses ONLY the string-surviving JSON-Schema keywords. ENFORCED: the `enum` closed domain on exchange; `pattern` shapes on cik (all digits — SEC CIKs are unpadded here) and ticker; and `minLength`/`maxLength` envelopes. NOT ENFORCED, by design: numeric ranges — a `type: number` BACKFIRES once a column is VARCHAR-melted (rejects 100%), so cik carries a digit `pattern`, not a numeric type; NULLs — ft_validate treats NULL and empty/whitespace as INVISIBLE, so nullable columns (exchange ~1.9%, ticker) still pass; and object-level `required` (documentation only). Free-text fields (company_name, corpus) carry LOOSE length envelopes with headroom, not tight formats. Derived from build/edgar.parquet: a pass-through on today's rows, a fail-closed tripwire for future drift (an unknown exchange, a non-digit cik, a shape break).
title: edgar
